summary: Iteration 2 focused testing: 11 new backend features (tiers, subscription, quota, brute-force, Stripe checkout, top-K) + frontend smoke (landing→register employer→dashboard→pricing→upgrade). 16/17 backend tests pass; 1 backend bug found in /api/payments/status when Stripe session cannot be retrieved (returns 500 instead of graceful state). All UI smoke checks pass.

minor.issue: Unhandled CheckoutError when Stripe stub cannot retrieve session ('No such checkout.session') → HTTP 500. Should catch exception and return current persisted payment_status (pending) or 502 with detail.

issues: Pricing tier cards have no skeleton/loader while /api/tiers loads – brief flash of header-only state. Minor.

action_items: Wrap stripe.get_checkout_status(...) in try/except in payment_status endpoint (server.py:483). On CheckoutError, return the current persisted tx state (e.g. {'payment_status': tx['payment_status'], 'status': tx['status'], 'tier_id': tx['tier_id']}) instead of bubbling a 500. The frontend polls this endpoint after Stripe redirect; a 500 will break the polling UX.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~650 lines and growing; consider splitting routes into modules (auth, employer, employee, admin, payments) to stay under 700 lines guideline.
POST /api/employer/checkout accepts payload as plain dict — should be a Pydantic model for validation/typed errors.
Brute-force protection key is `{ip}:{email}` — behind kubernetes ingress, request.client.host may be the proxy IP, weakening per-IP isolation. Consider trusting X-Forwarded-For (with care).

After 5 failed attempts in record_failed_attempt, count is reset to 0 alongside locked_until being set; subsequent failed attempts during lockout window correctly hit check_lockout. OK, but a comment would help maintainers.
Top-K pre-filter uses pure keyword overlap; jobs with 0 overlap can still be returned if profile_tokens empty. Acceptable for v1.
/api/tiers route is unauthenticated and inside _mount_routes() — fine for transparency, but if exposing future tier admin fields, lock it down.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@jobportal.ch / Admin123! (auto-seeded). Test users use unique TEST_ prefix and are cleaned up.
seed_data_creation: None outside test scope. Test fixtures create & delete their own employer/employee/bf users. Cleanup of UI test users + login_attempts done after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/iteration2_test.py -v` for iteration 2 regression. The one failing test (test_payment_status_owner) will pass once /api/payments/status wraps the Stripe call in try/except. Iteration 1 tests live in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and were not re-run per instructions. STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent appears to be a stub that creates session IDs which cannot later be retrieved via get_checkout_status; webhook path is the realistic flow.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE: In server.py payment_status (line ~483), `result = await stripe.get_checkout_status(session_id)` raises `emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError` when the underlying Stripe (test stub) returns 'No such checkout.session'. The exception propagates unhandled → FastAPI returns 500. REPRO: 1) POST /api/employer/checkout with valid tier_2+origin_url. 2) Immediately GET /api/payments/status/{session_id} → 500. MITIGATION: wrap the get_checkout_status call in try/except CheckoutError; on failure return the persisted tx fields so the FE poller still gets a structured response (payment_status='pending').
